title: Brownfield Requirements Handoff from Problem Framing
prompt: Read `./access-review-modernization-problem-framing.md`. Work only from that file. Do not ask follow-up questions. If you need assumptions, label them clearly and continue. Produce a first-release requirements set that preserves the chosen campaign-and-evidence-first coexistence direction, keeps the work in the requirements layer, preserves non-goals and unresolved questions, and shapes the result for downstream system-design or acceptance-criteria work.

assertions.name: stays-in-specification-phase
assertions.description: Output remains in the requirements layer and does not jump into architecture, migration sequencing, or implementation design

assertions.name: preserves-design-direction-boundary
assertions.description: Keeps the campaign-and-evidence-first coexistence direction as a release boundary instead of dissolving it into generic modernization scope

assertions.name: preserves-non-goals-and-open-questions
assertions.description: Carries forward explicit non-goals and unresolved questions rather than silently deciding them

assertions.name: avoids-invented-precision
assertions.description: Does not turn unsupported consistency or compatibility assumptions into hard quantitative requirements without labeling assumptions or open questions

assertions.name: prepares-downstream-handoff
assertions.description: Produces a requirements artifact shaped for downstream system-design or acceptance-criteria work
